title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sandwich Middle High School is under investigation for a student allegedly threatening two classmates, authorities confirmed.
The threat was detected by the district's safety app, GoGuardian, on a school-issued device, prompting police action.
The student involved has been removed from school, and the Sandwich Police and Cape DA continue the investigation.

title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington, 40, was convicted of first-degree murder in a 2015 Dorchester home invasion, faces life without parole.
Arrington was found guilty of multiple charges including murder and kidnapping after mistakenly attacking the wrong apartment.
Prosecutors used iPhone location data as key evidence in Arrington's retrial, setting a precedent for digital evidence use.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' began on June 8 with regional qualifiers and features about 20 New England contestants.
Notable competitors include Addy Herman and Noah Meunier, who have previous strong finishes and set a Guinness World Record in 2025.
New England's prominence in the sport is attributed to numerous ninja gyms and trainers like Jordan Thurston of Vitality Obstacle Fitness.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish fans visited Boston for the World Cup, engaging with locals and visiting landmarks like the Freedom Trail.
Scottish tourists socialized in Boston, visiting local spots and attending events, despite team's soccer loss to Morocco.
Meredith Goldstein invites stories of romance between locals and Scottish visitors, facilitated by the World Cup atmosphere.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' combines soccer and Shaolin kung fu, first released in Hong Kong in 2001, available on Paramount+, Tubi, Pluto TV, The Roku Channel.
'Diamantino' is a 2018 political satire about a Portuguese soccer star, streaming on Kanopy and Hoopla.
'United Passions', a film with a $30 million budget, grossed just $918 in its opening weekend, streaming on Tubi, The Roku Channel, Plex, The CW.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration is considering rolling back vessel speed restrictions designed to protect North Atlantic right whales.
North Atlantic right whales are endangered, with only about 380 remaining, facing threats primarily from vessel strikes.
Proposed changes include replacing static seasonal slow zones with dynamic real-time zones using whale-detecting technologies.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings, allowing one hit, in the Red Sox's 6-1 win over the Yankees.
Tolle took a perfect game into the sixth inning and struck out seven batters at Fenway Park.
Despite battling an illness, Tolle maintained a 2.78 ERA across 12 starts this season, becoming effective against the Yankees.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The Celtics could trade Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy and Yves Missi to strengthen their lineup.
To upgrade at center, Boston may target Mitchell Robinson, Robert Williams, or Zach Collins in free agency.
By trading Sam Hauser's $10 million contract, Boston could gain flexibility for strategic roster additions.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins acquired winger JJ Peterka from the Utah Mammoth in exchange for two first-round draft picks.
Peterka, 24, is a German forward known for his speed and scoring, averaging 26 goals over the past three seasons.
Despite a dip in production last season, the Bruins expect Peterka to improve with more power-play opportunities.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Detroit and Portland are interested in trading for Celtics' Jaylen Brown.
Boston reportedly seeks at least four first-round picks for Brown, but player combinations could be considered.
Portland and Detroit have assets like young players and draft picks that might tempt the Celtics.
